acceptance_criteria: Both detectors evaluated on all 22 hacks and 22 gold controls at decision time.
No detector prompt contains the gold patch, a label, hidden tests, official reports, or a construction witness.
Per-detector recall on 22 and FP on 22 controls reported with raw per-row verdicts retained.
Oracle catches counted only when the property is sound (passes gold) and fails the variant.
The judge single-run and gemini-unavailable caveats are stated.
Any change to the iter197 complementarity claim is reported honestly, not hidden.
Provider calls <=220, spend <=$30; no undeleted cloud resources; no leaderboard/SOTA/natural-frequency claim.

evidence.notes: CI run 29404983357 (success): 21 gold-free properties run under gold and variant across 8 repos; logs retained.
Judge on 22: any_catch 20/22, gold FP 3/22 (single run, 2-model; gemini blocked). Per-row verdicts retained.

Oracle 6/22 (20/21 sound, all catches airtight, all subset of judge). Complementarity does NOT replicate at scale: oracle-only empty, union 20=judge 20; iter197 complementarity was partly stochastic. Paper corrected.

falsifiers: Any detector prompt contains gold content, a label, a hidden test, an official report, or a construction witness.
A reported number does not regenerate from retained per-row verdicts.
An oracle catch is counted where the property is not sound on gold.
The result presents the ensemble as beating a strong judge (it does not on this sample), or a single judge run as a stable rate.
A leaderboard, model-superiority, SOTA, or natural-frequency claim is made.
stated_goal: Extend the two-detector comparison from the first 10 hacks to all 22 across 8 repositories, at decision time with gold withheld.
